mechanism_narrative: Sustained month-over-month payroll employment growth (PAYEMS) above 150,000 persons signals robust labor market momentum that increases consumer confidence and discretionary spending, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:February 2012 payroll growth of 227,000 persons indicates sustained hiring momentum
regime_hint: post-recession recovery with improving labor demand
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in fewer than 55% of months where PAYEMS growth exceeds 150,000, or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: The federal funds rate remaining at or near zero (FEDFUNDS < 0.25%) maintains an accommodative monetary stance that suppresses risk-free rates and incentivizes equity allocation, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS at 0.1% as of February 2012 reflects extended zero lower bound policy
failure_condition: SPX 60-day forward returns are not significantly positive (mean return < 1.5%) during periods when FEDFUNDS < 0.25%, or Rank IC < 0.01 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month decline in retail sales (RSAFS) below the prior 3-month average signals weakening consumer demand and reduced corporate earnings expectations, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:RSAFS at 407,805 million in February 2012 reflects consumer spending patterns
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 5-day returns in fewer than 50% of instances where RSAFS declines > 2% month-over-month, or signal generates false positives in > 45% of triggered cases.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO) growth accelerates above 0.5% month-over-month while unemployment (UNRATE) remains elevated above 8%, indicating supply-side capacity utilization gains without wage pressure, supporting profit margins and positive S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:INDPRO at 95.9054 and UNRATE at 8.3% in February 2012 reflect slack labor market with improving production
regime_hint: recovery phase with labor slack and rising capacity utilization
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in fewer than 58% of triggered instances, or the interaction effect (INDPRO × UNRATE) shows Rank IC < 0.04 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts (HOUST) rise above 700,000 annualized units while real GDP growth (GDPC1) remains positive, signaling construction sector strength and forward-looking economic confidence that drives positive S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:HOUST at 699,000 units in January 2012 near threshold for housing recovery signal
regime_hint: post-housing-crisis stabilization with emerging demand recovery
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60-day returns in fewer than 60% of instances where HOUST > 700,000 and GDPC1 growth is positive, or interaction term shows Rank IC < 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: The unemployment rate (UNRATE) declines by more than 0.3 percentage points over a 3-month period, signaling accelerating labor market tightening that reduces slack and increases wage pressure expectations, producing negative S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days due to inflation concerns.
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE at 8.3% in February 2012 remains elevated but subject to rapid tightening risk
regime_hint: late-cycle labor market tightening with inflation risk
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20-day returns in fewer than 48% of instances where UNRATE declines > 0.3pp over 3 months, or signal generates false positives in > 50% of triggered cases.

mechanism_narrative: Consumer price inflation (CPIAUCSL) accelerates above 2.5% year-over-year while the federal funds rate (FEDFUNDS) remains below 0.5%, creating a negative real rate environment that erodes purchasing power and signals potential future monetary tightening, producing negative S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:CPIAUCSL at 227.505 (January 2012) and FEDFUNDS at 0.1% reflect accommodative policy amid moderate inflation
regime_hint: monetary policy lag with rising inflation expectations
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20-day returns in fewer than 50% of triggered instances, or the interaction effect shows Rank IC < 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, indicating the mechanism is not reliably predictive.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO) declines month-over-month while payroll employment (PAYEMS) continues to grow, signaling a divergence where firms are reducing output but maintaining headcount, indicating cautious business sentiment and reduced earnings expectations that produce negative S&P 500 returns over 5 trading days.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO declines month-over-month AND PAYEMS growth < 100,000 persons
evidence_refs: fact:INDPRO at 95.9054 and PAYEMS at 132,697,000 in early 2012 show potential divergence risk
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 5-day returns in fewer than 52% of triggered instances, or the interaction signal shows Rank IC < 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window, indicating weak predictive power.
